title: Environmental Economists
description: Conduct economic analysis related to environmental protection and use of the natural environment, such as water, air, land, and renewable energy resources. Evaluate and quantify benefits, costs, incentives, and impacts of alternative options using economic principles and statistical techniques.

tasks.text: Write technical documents or academic articles to communicate study results or economic forecasts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct research on economic and environmental topics, such as alternative fuel use, public and private land use, soil conservation, air and water pollution control, and endangered species protection.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect and analyze data to compare the environmental implications of economic policy or practice alternatives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Assess the costs and benefits of various activities, policies, or regulations that affect the environment or natural resource stocks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Prepare and deliver presentations to communicate economic and environmental study results, to present policy recommendations, or to raise awareness of environmental consequences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop programs or policy recommendations to achieve environmental goals in cost-effective ways.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop economic models, forecasts, or scenarios to predict future economic and environmental outcomes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Demonstrate or promote the economic benefits of sound environmental regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct research to study the relationships among environmental problems and patterns of economic production and consumption.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform complex, dynamic, and integrated mathematical modeling of ecological, environmental, or economic systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write social, legal, or economic impact statements to inform decision makers for natural resource policies, standards, or programs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Teach courses in environmental economics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop programs or policy recommendations to promote sustainability and sustainable development.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop systems for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting environmental and economic data.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write research proposals and grant applications to obtain private or public funding for environmental and economic studies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine the exhaustibility of natural resources or the long-term costs of environmental rehabilitation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor or analyze market and environmental trends.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Develop environmental research project plans, including information on budgets, goals, deliverables, timelines, and resource requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify and recommend environmentally friendly business practices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Interpret indicators to ascertain the overall health of an environment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
